title: Watershed Manifest
description: A registry of repositories assigned to layers, with dependency direction enforced mechanically. Dependencies flow downhill only: a repo may depend on repos in strictly lower layers. Sideways and uphill dependencies are illegal. The manifest is the durable surface; the ecosystem it describes is the instance.

basin.description: The repo every path drains toward. The frozen kernel. Must appear in repos at the lowest layer.

index.description: 0 is the basin layer. Higher numbers are downstream consumers.

rule.description: One sentence stating what this layer may and may not do. Human-readable statement of the invariant the CI check enforces.

dependable.description: When false, no repo anywhere may declare a dependency on a repo in this layer. For layers that interact with the ecosystem only through ledgers at runtime (observers, orchestrators): position in the ordering grants no code-dependency rights in either direction. Layer 0 must be dependable.

layers.description: Layer indices must be unique and contiguous from 0.

id.description: Versioned vocabulary identifier, e.g. temporal@1, evidence-tiers@1. Version bumps are new ids; extensions are immutable once published.

layer.description: Extensions must be defined at layer 0. Any other value fails validation. The field exists so the failure is explicit rather than implicit.

defined_in.description: Repo id where the canonical spec lives.

spec.description: Path or URL to the spec document.

target.description: Declared target state, not yet actual. The edge must still be legal (downhill, dependable, non-archived); the flag only stops the manifest from asserting a present-tense fact that is not true. Remove the flag when the dependency becomes real.

depends_on.description: Repo ids this repo imports, links, or reads at build or runtime. Every listed id must exist in repos and sit at a strictly lower dependable layer. Empty array is a claim, not an omission. An entry may be an object with target: true to declare an aspirational edge; a manifest containing any target edge describes a forecast, not a state, and the checker says so.

consumes_extensions.description: Every listed extension id must exist in extensions. Consuming vocabulary is the only legal sideways-looking relationship, and it resolves downhill because extensions live at layer 0.

status.description: frozen: normative surface immutable - the spec, vocabulary, and signature roots, anything a downstream verifier's result depends on; no dependencies; conformance fixtures, CI config, and tooling changes are permitted, because they are the frozen thing's immune system, not the frozen thing. active: normal development. incubating: layer assignment provisional, excluded from downstream depends_on. legacy: may be depended on, may not add dependencies. archived: nothing may depend on it.

release.description: Current tagged release or commit hash.

produces.description: What the repo emits: ledgers, graphs, reports, signatures. For publication-layer entries, the citation targets.
